column5: Gas Chromatography equipment manufactured by Bruker Scientific
column29: National Institute of Allergy & Infectious Diseases (NIAID)’s Laboratory of Clinical Immunology and Microbiology (LCIM) (collectively Program)  requires the use of mass spectrometers (MS) and gas chromatographs/gas chromatography (GC) for the examination, collection, and identification of data from samples because one of their current projects will assess the metabolic response to pollution, specifically, assessing the metabolic pathways needed for bacteria metabolism, and then assess how these pathways are altered in response to anti-microbial challenge (both prescription drugs as well as pollutant insults).  Traditional MS devices require follow up evaluations for any metabolite identified – meaning any potential target of interest will require additional time, money, and resources to confirm it the identification of the metabolite is correct.  With the new technology ION mobility, which assesses both the metabolite’s mass and its 3D shape in space, definitive identification can be performed on all metabolites assessed at first pass - thus Program requires purchasing an Bruker gas chromatography equipment that can be combined with their existing Bruker ION mobility outfitted instrument and Bruker mass spectrometer.

column5: Assay Kits from Olink Proteomics Inc.
column29: National Institute of Allergy & Infectious Diseases (NIAID)’s Viral Pathogenesis and Evolution Section (VPES)within Laboratory of Infectious Diseases (LID) (collectively “Program”) is developing broadly neutralizing universal influenza vaccine, and must study the identification of diagnostic and predictive biomarkers for vaccinee efficacy, viral infection, vaccine-induced memory responses, length of viral shedding, symptom severity, and progression of disease. In order to achieve this mission, Program requires proprietary assay kits from Olink Proteomics Inc. (“Olink”)that are capable of measuring 96 different immune and inflammatory protein mediators in nasal, serum, and pulmonary tissue samples from animal model and human studies developing and testing universal influenza vaccine candidates. These studies are part of previous and on-going studies examining immune responses in vaccination and influenza viral challenge human studies.

column5: VT1000 S Vibrating Blade Microtome by Leica Microsystems
column29:
National Institute of Allergy & Infectious Diseases (NIAID)’s Division of Intramural Research (DIR)’s 
Laboratory of Host Immunity and Microbiome (LHIM) (collectively “Program”) is doing research to assess the consequences of environmental stress, such as infection, nutrition, and pollution, on host immunity and predisposition to inflammatory disorders in the context of fetal development, early life, and in adults.  To achieve this, Program is employing a broad range of neuroscience and immunology tools and techniques from molecular biology, histology assays, single-cell sequencing, mouse surgery, mouse engineering, calcium imaging, infection models and immune cell profiling - with one particular aspect of the research involving imaging live and fixed mouse brain tissue.  Thus, Program requires the purchase of a vibratome system, specifically the Leica VT1000S Vibrating Blade Microtome System in order to slice sections of mouse brain tissue for their research.

column5: Cell and Virion Analysis Equipment (CytoFLEX S and CytoFLEX SRT)
column29: Research programs in the Laboratory of Viral Diseases (LVD) explore fundamental aspects of cell and molecular biology, viral pathogenesis, and viral immunology within the context of a diverse group of medically important viruses that includes human/simian immunodeficiency viruses, poxviruses, herpesviruses, papillomaviruses, coronaviruses, influenza viruses, alphaviruses and flaviviruses. The goals of these research programs are to create knowledge that increases a basic biological understanding of these pathogens and their interactions with their hosts and to generate new strategies for preventing and treating viral diseases.  Dr. Tijana Ivanovic, an investigator recently recruited by the Laboratory of Viral Diseases, requires laboratory equipment capable of detecting and isolating viral particles to conduct her research. The intended outcome of this acquisition is to procure a cell cytometer and sorter system capable of reliably and unambiguously detect virions, which are orders of magnitude smaller than cells.

column6: 334511 - Search, Detection, Navigation, Guidance, Aeronautical, and Nautical System and Instrument Manufacturing
column29: The Observatory is the National Water Level Observation Network (NWLON), which supports the regional Physical Oceanographic Real-Time System (PORTS), U.S. Tsunami Warning Centers, and long-term sea levels rise studies. The Septentrio GNSS receivers are co-located with the NWLON tide gauges and are used to monitor the vertical stability of the tide gauge; the rate of rise or fall of the platform (e.g., dock, elevated structure, bulkhead) which the tide gauge is located. Normally very small, these measurements are compared with regional land motion measurements, and any difference indicates the observatory is moving relative to the surrounding land. This difference impacts the accuracy of our long-term water level measurements, and allows researchers to better resolve long-term sea level change for all U.S. coastal regions. Observatories that show large movements are flagged for relocation and the data annotated for stability errors. The GNSS receivers allow us to monitor these motions on a daily basis and record a long-term record of the tide gauge movement. The National Ocean Service, Center for Operational Oceanographic Products and Services (CO-OPS) currently has fifteen GNSS receivers, which have undergone extensive testing in operational deployments. Due to the complexity and size of CO-OPS’ typical observatory, five additional GNSS receivers are required to provide 10+ years data coverage at NWLON locations. CO-OPS’ existing equipment standards, which includes mounting systems, hardware integration, power systems, and other infrastructure, mandates continues use of Septentrio GNSS receivers, specifically the PolaRx5 model, which are required to integrate with our established workflow and existing hardware.

column5: Tunable Laser for Entangled Photon Source
column6: 333310 - Commercial and Service Industry Machinery Manufacturing
column17: NATIONAL INSTITUTE OF STANDARDS AND TECHNOLOGY
column29:
An integrated entangled photon source is an important device for quantum communication and networking. The entangled photon source based on SiC micro­ring is a promising solution of such device. Currently, the quantum communication and network project at the Information Technology Laboratory (ITL) at the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) is developing that kind of entangled photon source. 

The NIST ITL is developing an entangled photon source at telecom band (around 1550 nm) based on SiC micro-ring.  To support this work,  ITL requires a tunable laser for the pump of the entangled photon source. The tunable laser must be continuously tunable around telecom band without mode-hops. Further, the output at fiber output must be larger than 11 mW and wavelength accuracy better than 150 pm.

EasySep kits are fast and easy, and isolated cells are ready to use. They are efficient and we can perform sequential or simultaneous cell isolations with these kits. The column-free design and minimal sample handling is required for safety and to reduce the risk of exposure to dangerous pathogens. column5: Automated Stereotaxic Injection System for Performing Intracranial Injections in Mice
column29:
This requirement is for equipment that will be used by the National Institute of Neurological Disorders and Stroke (NINDS) to perform intracranial injections and to make cranial windows into mouse brains. These experiments will directly test the effects of alterations in neural circuitry in various animal models of Parkinson’s Disease. 

The goal for this project is to determine how basal ganglia and midbrain dopamine circuits are altered in animal models of Parkinson’s Disease. The neural manipulations will be conducted and the movement of the mouse quantified in both normal and Parkinsonian mice. In order to generate the Parkinson’s disease model, NINDS will express alpha synuclein in midbrain dopamine neurons. Alpha synuclein is a protein that has been associated with cell death among dopamine neurons. In order to infect
dopamine neurons, NINDS will need to perform intracranial injections in mice.

To precisely target the midbrain dopamine neurons with an injection needle requires substantial experience and can occur with variable success between animals. However, using a robot guided injection method will allow NINDS to more consistently target
brain structures of interest. Therefore, this is an essential piece of equipment. NINDS requires a high precision stereotaxic instrument that will be used for accurate targeting of the optical fibers in the mouse brain. Because the mouse brain is smaller than a rats, the precision required for targeting specific locations is higher than under other conditions.

The Epigenetic and Stem Cell Unit (ESC), Translational Gerontology Branch (TGB), National Institute on Aging (NIA) require a transgenic knock in anima. These mice are generated with a mouse strain with Cre inserted at CD48 gene locus. CD48 is a gene that is only expressed in differentiated cells and not in hematopoietic stem cell (HSC).
Cre is a recombinase that can carry out site specific recombination. By using this mouse model, after introducing regulatory element flanked by Cre target sites, the regulatory element will only execute its function in HSC and will be removed after HSC differentiation by Cre mediated recombination. The frequency of correct knockin in mice is usually low which makes successful generation of this mouse strain challenging, with the risk of failure.

NINDS seeks fundamental knowledge about the brain and nervous system and to use that knowledge to reduce the burden of neurological disease for all people.  The Tracking OCT System with OCT2 Module is an expandable diagnositic imaging platform that combines scanning laser fundus imaging with high-resolution OCT. This package also includes the OCT2 Module, providing more than twice the scanning speed of the Spectralis. This increased scanning speed supports efficient clinical workflows when performing comprehensive glaucoma evaluations. This model will replace an outdated generation of equipment, thus improving imaging capabilities, enhance clinical decision-making, and preserving patient data. This instrument is crucial for research programs and has been used to support collaborations with extramural projects. If no waiver is granted and NIDA is not able to procure a new Tracking OCT System with OCT2 Module, and the current equipment malfunctions, then TNS and NIC’s work could be delayed or even halted. These delays will also adversely impact all future worked planned. Furthermore, The current OCT is an outdated model by Heidelberg Engineering and requires replacement. column5: Procurement of radical Ox071 contrast agent for use in electron paramagnetic resonance imaging.
column6: 325199 - All Other Basic Organic Chemical Manufacturing
column29:
The NCI seeks to procure 20 grams of Oxo71 EPR imaging agent to fulfill the needs of NCI researchers performing metabolic imaging research in collaboration with the CCR Metabolomics Core. 

The Metabolomics Core facilitates access to a pre-clinical electron paramagnetic resonance (EPR) imaging instrument that is designed, built, and operated by the NCI Radiation Biology Branch. EPR imaging is used to make spatially resolved measurements of tissue oxygen concentration in the tissues of live animals, using paramagnetic trityl probes Oxo63 and Oxo71 as contrast agents. EPR imaging can be used to quantitatively determine tissue oxygenation because both the spin lattice relaxation time (T1 = 1/R1) and spin-spin relaxation time (T2 = 1/R2) or paramagnetic electrons are shortened in the presence of oxygen. 

Recently, the Metabolomics Core determined that Oxo71, a deuterated form of Oxo63, provides superior oxygen imaging characteristics compared to its predecessor. Oxo71 can measure a wider dynamic range of oxygen concentrations in vivo. This agent will be used to fulfill the needs of the NCI Urinary Oncology Branch, Radiation Biology Branch, and any other NCI researchers performing metabolic imaging research in collaboration with the NCI Metabolomics Core.

column29:
To acquire forty-four (44) specified agents for replenishment and expansion of the Mechanism Interrogation PlatE (MIPE) screening library.  

The National Center for Advancing Translational Sciences (NCATS) at the National Institutes of Health (NIH), has a multidisciplinary team that developed a high-capacity robotic platform to screen drug combinations. In just a couple of days, the robots can perform a drug screen that would take human researchers months to complete. The robotic system performs the automated assays using diseased cells that are placed on plates in tiny indentations called wells. Cells are treated in each well with a unique combination of drugs, and the assays reveal the effects of the drugs on each group of cells.

One feature that makes the platform stand out from previous combination drug screening efforts is the drug library it uses, called the Mechanism Interrogation PlatE (MIPE). This library not only enables NCATS to find compound combinations that can go rapidly into clinical trials, but also to explore the biological processes that create the enhanced effect of a drug combination in a cell, leading to new drug targets. The evolution of the MIPE library requires regular updating with new agents. The MIPE 6.0 expansion will incorporate a number of new agents and further the potential of chemogenomic screening at NCATS.

column5: VANTASTAR PLATE READER INSTRUMENT AND COMPONENTS
column29: After testing multiple plate readers from various companies the BMG VantaSTAR was found to be compatible with already purchased instrument extensions (ClarioSTAR and OmegaSTAR in lab currently). Along with instrument consistency, the VantaSTAR has novel engineered capabilities that in-house comparisons show are over 10x more sensitive in reliably detecting (signal/noise) fluorescence compared to the top-line Neo2 instrument. The standard configuration of the VantaSTAR is capable of detecting substantial fluorescence in the far-red channels, which is not the case for other instruments tested. This engineering enhancement has to do with the light-path and sensor positions and is not available on any other instruments at this time. Not only is this instrument more sensitive for fluorescence, it is also 100x more sensitive at detecting chemical luminescence. The flash chemiluminescence detection settings are also greatly enhanced by a newly developed algorithm (EDR Flash Gain Adjustment) that allows the instrument to autogain flash-chemilum samples that no other instrument is capable of. The VantaSTAR also comes with environmental control options and an appropriately designed in-instrument autoinjection system that are well implemented and are absent on most other plate reader models.

column17: NATIONAL INSTITUTE OF STANDARDS AND TECHNOLOGY
column29:
The NIST Center for Neutron Research (NCNR) provides specialized laboratory equipment for staff and neutron scattering users to characterize materials of interest. These capabilities help neutron users to best characterize their materials and hence produce world-class scientific research from their neutron scattering measurements. Currently, NIST does have the capability to determine the masses of molecular fragments or their distribution.  To achieve this capability NIST requires an inline mass spectrometer that is compatible with the existing Netzsch STA 449 F1 Jupiter Simultaneous Thermal Analyzer (STA) to automate simultaneous experiments so users can correlate the masses of the evolved species and determine the chemical compositions.

Purpose/Objective
A key mode of operation for this instrument is to take materials to temperatures where they start to decompose or release small molecules such as water and adsorbed gas species. Knowledge of those evolved molecular species allows for the determination of the stability and thermodynamics of those materials and infer crucial chemical information, and there are usually multiple types of species being ejected over a range of temperatures. NIST cannot currently determine the masses of these molecular fragments or their distribution

column5: Procartaplex Platinum 42 plex human cytokine assay kit
column29: The Antiviral Program for Pandemics (APP) of the NCATS requires the purchase of Procartaplex Platinum 42 plex human cytokine assay kit. NCATS, part of the Tox21 is involved in research of the toxicity effects of various environmental chemicals on the human cardiovascular system. NCATS has developed a human cell model that will be probed with a set of chosen chemicals and will then be assayed for the effects of these compounds. We are developing a method to detect gene expression changes in treated cultured human cell lines that will be cost and time effective. These changes may be correlated with various phenotypic assays that other groups within NCATS will have performed. We wish to find a method that will bridge these results, the use of multiplexed Luminex bead-based assays to look at actual protein level changes of various cytokines in these same treated culture human cell lines. This assay will provide a cost-effective way to compare multiple (42) protein levels in treated cell lines concurrently. As this is a multiplexed assay, the method allows conservation of resources for NCATS. The timeline for the assay also provides a means to perform more data acquisition than with other assays. The primary reason for the purchase of this particular assay kit is the bundling of the particular analytes in this kit.

The FAA Contracting Officer posted a source sought announcement on SAM.GOV for any manufactures that could meet the requirement of a curved staircase wheel chair lift under the Buy American Act Requirement. 
On closing no responses or inquiries were received. 
The FAA Engineer in conjunction with the Procurement Team consisting of the Contracting  Officer and BOA awarded Contractor conducted a market survey which included discussions with elevator distributors, internet searches.
The Market Survey yielded only two (2) manufactures that could meet the requirement/criteria of a continuous curved non-wall mounted lift. The companies that produce such a product are Garaventa and Savaria. Both manufactures produce their assemblies in Canada. Garaventa steel is manufactured, and their steel shapes are milled in Taiwan. The material origin limitation has been an inability to identify a US Steel Mill that can produce to specifications. Savaria’s steel is manufactured in either Germany or the Czech Republic. The FAA Contract Specialist conducted a market survey on the GSA Supply list and GSA Library and yielded no manufactures to meet the requirement. Further calls to smaller manufactures of lifts: 1. Gillespi Corporation and 2. Handi-Ramp both did not have products that could meet the interior rail requirement for operation.
 No Domestic product has been identified that can meet the Government requirements for the curved lift.

Refrigerated heating recirculating baths are required for use with various neutron scattering equipment at the NIST Center for Neutron Research (NCNR). The fleet of refrigerated heating recirculating baths available at NCNR are aging and in need of replacement. These refrigerated heating recirculating baths are necessary to control temperature of in-situ experiment stations specifically used in neutron scattering experiments at the Small Angle Neutron Scattering Instruments (SANS) and the Neutron Spin-Echo Instrument (NSE). 
Purpose
Control temperature of in-situ experiment stations specifically used in neutron scattering experiments at the Small Angle Neutron Scattering Instruments (SANS) and the Neutron Spin-Echo Instrument (NSE).

The Antiviral Program for Pandemics (APP) aims to develop safe and effective antivirals to combat SARS-CoV-2. APP will accelerate the development of a portfolio of promising antivirals that can quickly pivot to combat future pandemics. A microwave chemical synthesis system allows reactions to be completed in a fraction of the time and will help the APP group develop potential candidates against future pandemic threats by aiding in the synthesis of potential lead compounds to be tested in biological assays, as well as in vivo animal efficacy models.

The purpose of this acquisition is to acquire two (2) of the Biotage Initiator+ Robot Sixty Microwave System or Equal that meet all salient characteristics.

The CBGC has the first generation of 10X Genomics Chromium Controllers that were acquired in 2018. Given that the number of single-cell projects at the National Institute on Aging-Intramural Research Program (NIA-IRP) has grown over the years, it is now necessary to upgrade the Chromium Controllers. The National Institute on Aging requires a controller that offers high flexibility allowing not only to run high-throughput single-cell assays, but also the completion of ongoing projects with previously purchased low-throughput kits. Many Intramural Research Program (IRP) labs including Laboratory of Genetics & Genomics (LGG), Laboratory of Molecular Biology & Immunology (LMBI), Translational Gerontology Branch (TGB), Laboratory of Cardiovascular Science (LCS), Laboratory of Clinical Investigation (LCI) and others will benefit if this instrument is added to the CBGC facility. CBGC has other instruments and expertise required for the 10X Genomics Chromium X to work seamlessly such as the Illumina Novaseq 6000 sequencer as well as trained staff for running both the wet-lab protocols and data analysis pipelines.

Approval is requested to purchase construction materials Arc Fault (AFCI) receptacles that are manufactured in
China and provided by a distributor located in MA. This component will be installed as part of the
N40085-20-C-0025 contract for the B488 Renovation project at Naval Submarine Base New
London in Groton, CT. The prime contractor, Nutmeg, has requested a post-award determination
of inapplicability of the Buy American Statute  based on the nonavailability of
construction material AFCI receptacles required for the performance of the contract valued at
approximately $41,000 (1,200 AFCI receptacles at $23.38 to $34.52 per unit). Based on the Government’s review of the supporting data provided by Nutmeg and the findings of fact and reasoning discussed above, it is determined that the construction material AFCI receptacles manufactured in China and distributed through a supplier in MA (or one from one of the other
domestic distributors that acquire AFCI receptacles manufactured in China) is currently the only
available AFCI receptacles that meets the applicable requirements in modifications P00013 and
P00016 and should, therefore, be allowed as a construction material on this project. Since the AFCI
receptacles are not mined, produced, or manufactured in the United States in sufficient and
reasonably available commercial quantities of a satisfactory quality, the nonavilablility to the Buy
American Statute applies.

column29:
The project consists of renovating an existing building and addition of two (2) wings to the existing structure, Building 434 located at Beltsville Agricultural Research Center, Beltsville, Maryland. Demolition includes but is not limited to, removal of finishes, windows, doors, concrete slab, masonry walls, wood walls, ceilings, roofing, plumbing, electrical, and stairs in the existing building. Renovation includes but is not limited to creating an office space, restrooms, mechanical spaces, storage space, foot washing stations, and providing mechanical service to the new wings. Renovation will convert the existing structure to a 12 room, 4,017 SF, support and admin space for the poultry housed in the new wings. The two (2) new wings will be new metal frame construction with four (4) rooms totaling 6,875 SF which will serve as a poultry quarantine facility for chickens and turkeys. These new wings will
include evaporative cooling climate controls. Site work includes but is not limited to construction of a temporary access road, improvement of the existing ring road, construction of paved parking spaces, concrete slabs on either side of the wings, grading, sediment and erosion control measures, and landscaping.

This IDIQ procurement is to acquire 15.5-KV to 362-KV circuit breakers.  A circuit breaker is an electrical switch designed to protect an electrical circuit from damage caused by overcurrent/overload or short circuit. Its basic function is to interrupt current flow after protective relays detect a fault.  Every substation has multiple circuit breakers.  They are used on transmission lines, transformer lines,  reactor banks, and capacitor banks.

column29: The USGS, California Water Science Center in Sacramento, California, supporting the Water Mission Area requires the purchase of new data logger/transmitters, referred to as EnviSense, for telemetering environmental sensor data. The EnviSense data logger/transmitters have been custom designed and engineered in partnership between the USGS National Innovation Center and Carnegie Mellon University. The EnviSense connects to SDI-12 compatible environmental sensors used by the USGS and transmits those sensed data in real-time using a Low Power Wide Area Network (LPWAN) technology called LoRa. The LoRa transmitter is embedded in the hardware of the EnviSense and the firmware code was written to use LoRa for transmitting the sensor data. The Department of the Interior has a government-use license on this technology.

They are used on transmission lines, transformer lines,  reactor banks, and capacitor banks.

Acoustic Doppler Current Profilers (ADCPs) are the standard in current measurement, replacing older, more man hour intensive technologies. Current measurements throughout the water column are an integral part of this field program, and in
particular, the ability to take multiple, precise measurement of the currents within a single meter with high frequency is necessary to resolve wave velocities and turbulence in the vicinity of artificial reef structures. A 1 MHz unit is required for the water depth and desired depth resolution at the proposed deployment site. The size and shape of the instrument itself is important for mounting considerations and can significantly affect the measurements of other instruments and must be considered. High resolution measurements of acoustic backscatter and vertical velocity are critical to studies of turbulence and sediment transport. Nortek is the only manufacturer of the 5-Beam Acoustic Doppler Profiler measuring instruments with the required salient characteristics.

column29: A handheld Raman spectrometer is critical to the mission for three separate programs of the Infrastructure Materials Group in the Engineering Laboratory (EL) at NIST.  A handheld Raman spectrometer is used for the characterization of materials in the field before and after long-term weathering or hazard events. Field-based Raman characterization is needed to obtain molecular bonding information in order to classify materials and understand degradation modes.  Specifically, the “Engineered Materials for Resilient Infrastructure” program involves the field characterization of infrastructure materials, including weathered polymers, polymer composites, and concrete. The “Earthquake Risk Reduction” program investigates the durability and field performance of fiber reinforced composites (e.g. “Reliability of Fiber Reinforced Composites in Infrastructure” project). Additionally, the “Net Zero Energy Research” program requires the field characterization of solar panel materials within solar arrays at NIST and at different sites across the United States.

column29: As per the contract, it was required that contractor install a dual tone doorbell to distinguish at which entry the doorbell was being used. It was unknown before award that it would not be possible to find an American sourced doorbell that met these requirements. After a thorough search done by the contractor, the government was notified that a dual tone doorbell sourced in America was difficult to find and contractor requested the use of a dual tone doorbell sourced in China. The Government conducted their own research to find there are no such comparable products produced in the United States that meet the requirements for this application. Research was conducted by an online search of multiple manufacturers, Enclosure (2), and also by speaking with a manufacturer from Nicor. The representative from Nicor stated that practically all units are manufactured overseas and that it would be impossible to find a dual tone doorbell manufactured in America.

column6: 334515 - Instrument Manufacturing for Measuring and Testing Electricity and Electrical Signals
column17: NATIONAL INSTITUTE OF STANDARDS AND TECHNOLOGY
column29:
The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) Information Technology Laboratory (ITL) is in the process of expanding its applied cybersecurity research efforts related to 5G cybersecurity. This work spans a broad area covering topics associated with both traditional information technology cybersecurity as well as cybersecurity for mobile networks requiring radio frequency (RF) isolation. This environment ensures that its interior volume is shielded from signals radiated from outside the chamber. Such a shielded research facility can come in the form of either an anechoic chamber or a reverberation chamber. 

In an anechoic chamber, the walls and ceiling inside the chamber are equipped with absorbing material such that signals radiated from within the chamber are absorbed and do not scatter (ideally). In a reverberation chamber, the opposite is true. The walls and ceiling in a reverberation chamber are designed to be reflective (metallic) at radio frequencies of interest. Most anechoic and reverberation chambers are designed, built, and used for testing pursuant to specific electromagnetic capability (EMC) standards (e.g., IEC 61000-4-3). As this chamber will be used for wireless communications research, it is neither feasible nor reasonable to specify the exact application and/or measurements that will take place within the chamber.

The purpose of this requirement is to obtain a free-standing RF Shielded Chamber for research purposes. Installation shall take place at the National Cybersecurity Center of Excellence facility in Rockville, MD. This is a firm fixed price requirement.
